title: AI Strategist
must_haves: Led complex client-facing projects (AI / technology consulting / fast-moving enterprise)
Own business outcomes end-to-end; credible with C-suite and ops teams
Comfortable with Python, SQL or similar ('technical enough to be dangerous')
hard_blockers: Role centres on enterprise delivery / technology-consulting track record (F500 telecom/healthcare/insurance/manufacturing) which Ryan lacks
First-hire-in-London framing implies a senior, self-directing operator who has run enterprise engagements before
met_strengths: Genuine agentic-AI fluency (building agents with Claude Code since 2023)
Strong with ambiguity, founder-adjacent ownership, turning vague priorities into workstreams
Excellent communicator across investors, community, PR; can hold a room
Technical-enough literacy (node ops, Amazon Alexa data analysis) though no Python/SQL production work
one_line_verdict: Real agentic-AI + ownership fit and London is the right geo, but the role is built for an enterprise-delivery/consulting operator with F500 client engagements — Ryan's community-led-growth background is adjacent, not core.
fetch_note: Confirmed open via Ashby posting API (job-board/Distyl). London is the genuine first-hire role, distinct from US AI Strategist listings. Requirements pulled from full descriptionHtml.
